hooks.prompt: Review the staged changes and test files in tests/ for quality issues. Check for: (1) Coverage gaps — are there important code paths without corresponding tests? (2) Missing edge cases — are error conditions, boundary values, and empty inputs tested? (3) Race conditions — are there shared mutable state or concurrent operations without proper synchronization? (4) Phantom tests — tests that don't actually verify behavior, such as asserting True, asserting on constants, or testing only the test setup. (5) Documentation — do new or significantly changed Python modules have docstrings explaining their purpose? Do public classes and functions have docstrings? Report specific file names and test names for any issues found. Return {"ok": true} if quality is solid, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issues"} to block the commit.
